# #10197 — agent stability: evidence

Memory watchdog + crash-recovery/soak harness for the local + cloud agent. This
PR delivers the **runtime + CLI/Node** stability story end-to-end with real,
runnable tests, plus the cross-platform watchdog parity code; the
device-connected and cloud-cluster lanes are wired but their live runs are
explicitly gated on hardware/cluster not present in this environment (marked
below, per `PR_EVIDENCE.md`).

## What shipped

| Deliverable | Where | Proof |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Memory watchdog** | `packages/agent/src/runtime/memory-watchdog.ts`, wired in `runtime/eliza.ts` | Restarts cleanly via `requestRestart()` (exit 75 → supervisor) when RSS holds ≥ threshold for N samples; never `process.exit`. 12 unit tests. |
| **Supervisor crash-recovery** | `packages/app-core/scripts/lib/restart-guard.mjs` (extracted from `run-node.mjs`) | 5 guard unit tests + **2 real-process integration tests** that drive the actual `run-node.mjs` with a fake child (real exit-75) → relaunch + crash-loop abort. |
| **Soak / leak detector** | `packages/agent/src/runtime/memory-soak.ts` | `runSoak()` forces GC + reports heap slope; 2 tests prove it flags a retained-allocation leak and reports a steady workload as flat. |
| **Umbrella + scoreboard** | `packages/app-core/scripts/stability-suite.mjs` (`stability:suite`) | One command **induces** crash → recovery + a leak workload, writes `10197-agent-stability-scoreboard.md`, exits non-zero on any failure. |
| **iOS watchdog parity** | `packages/app-core/platforms/ios/App/App/AgentWatchdog.swift` (+ `AppDelegate.swift`) | Liveness poll via the in-process `ElizaBunRuntime` bridge (no TCP port) + 3-strike detection mirroring Android, emitting a bounded restart-request (max 5/backoff). `swiftc -typecheck` passes (exit 0) vs the iOS 16 SDK (Capacitor stubbed). Device-run gated (below). **Caveat:** to auto-recover end-to-end the renderer must consume the `eliza:local-agent-restart-requested` signal and call `ElizaBunRuntime.start(...)` — the Swift side raises the signal; wiring the renderer consumer is the remaining device-side step. |

## Real, reviewed results

### Test suites (all green)
- `packages/agent` — `memory-watchdog.test.ts` **12 passed**, `memory-soak.test.ts` **2 passed**.
- `packages/app-core` — `restart-guard.test.mjs` **5 passed**, `run-node-supervisor.test.mjs` **2 passed** (real child processes).

### Umbrella scoreboard (`node packages/app-core/scripts/stability-suite.mjs`, exit 0)
```
[stability] PASS supervisor-recovery {"inducedRestarts":2,"recovered":true,"recoveryLatencyMs":4526}
[stability] PASS crash-loop-guard    {"spawnsBeforeAbort":6,"aborted":true}
[stability] PASS memory-soak         {"leakingSlopeMbPerIter":3.815,"steadySlopeMbPerIter":-0.095,"steadyPeakRssMb":122.48,"leakingGrowthMb":53.42}
```
The committed scoreboard is `10197-agent-stability-scoreboard.md` (regenerated by the suite).

## Acceptance criteria

- [x] **Single command, non-zero on failure** — `stability:suite` exits non-zero on any crash-without-recovery, unbounded RSS growth, or failed restart (verified by inverting each lane locally).
- [x] **Committed markdown scoreboard** — per-lane verdicts + induced-restart/recovery-latency/peak-RSS/heap-slope metrics.
- [x] **Memory watchdog evidence (mechanism)** — watchdog fires `[MemoryWatchdog]` + `requestRestart` at the threshold (unit-proven); the supervisor relaunch it depends on is proven by the real-process supervisor-recovery lane. A full boot-driven RSS-past-threshold run that writes `<stateDir>/telemetry/memory/latest.json` needs a booted agent with a model — **gated** (see below).
- [ ] **Device-connected evidence (Android/iOS)** — **N/A here: no physically-connected phone.** Android watchdog already exists; the iOS `AgentWatchdog.swift` parity is added but a real on-device crash→recovery capture (screenshot + recording + `adb logcat`/iOS console) requires a connected device. Lane: `packages/app/scripts/android-e2e.mjs --serial` / an iOS-device equivalent.
- [ ] **Cloud evidence (chainsaw kill/OOM → health restored)** — **N/A here: no k8s cluster.** `restart: unless-stopped` is in compose; the chainsaw lane (`packages/cloud/infra/cloud/tests/`) needs a live cluster to kill a pod and assert recovery.
- [ ] **Real-LLM soak trajectory** — **N/A here: no live model key/budget in this environment.** The `runSoak` harness accepts a `workload` that drives a real message-loop turn; the deterministic CI leak test uses a synthetic allocation workload. A live-model soak should run in the scenario-runner lane.

## Reviewer notes
- The supervisor refactor (`restart-guard.mjs`) is behavior-identical to the prior inline guard — same constants (`MAX_RESTARTS_IN_WINDOW=5`, `RESTART_WINDOW_MS=60000`), now unit-testable.
- The watchdog is **opt-in** (`ELIZA_MEMORY_WATCHDOG`), default off → production boot is byte-identical until enabled. Env documented in `packages/agent/CLAUDE.md`.
- No second restart mechanism was added anywhere — the watchdog and both device watchdogs all funnel through the existing `requestRestart()`/supervisor (CLI) or the host relaunch entrypoint (device).
